page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz with an x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 4 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. It includes 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The CPU cache configuration consists of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 3 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside weaker multi-core scaling. The single-core performance is characterized by an average Geekbench score of 1563.0 and an average stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1858.18. Multi-core performance is weaker, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 3303.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 3853.92. Memory performance shows a split profile: large block write bandwidth is average at 25.91 GB/sec, while read-only bandwidth is weak, registering 30.29 GB/sec for large blocks and 209.91 GB/sec for cached small blocks. Database operations and Redis SET operations also fall into the weaker performance tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r7.xlarge offers high memory density per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require heavy parallel CPU processing. The presence of hardware-accelerated instruction sets, such as AVX-512 VNNI and AES cryptography, assists in specialized tasks like small-scale LLM inference, where it achieves average speeds of 103.36 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M model. This instance is best suited for memory-bound applications, caching layers, small databases, and static web serving.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor, it provides 8.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage, relying on network-attached storage. Baseline network bandwidth is 3 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1563.0, but weaker multi-core scaling, as evidenced by a multi-core score of 3303.0. Memory write bandwidth is average at 25.91 GB/sec, while read bandwidth is relatively weak. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for caching layers, small databases, static web serving, and lightweight memory-bound applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high memory density for memory-bound applications.
